commit.message:
Add Unicode scripts, blocks, categories, binary properties support (#120)

Add JDK-compatible \p{...} syntax for:
- Is prefix for scripts: \p{IsLatin}, \p{IsGreek}, etc.
- Is prefix for categories: \p{IsL}, \p{IsLu}, etc.
- Is prefix for binary properties: \p{IsAlphabetic}, \p{IsUppercase},
  \p{IsEmoji}, etc. (20 properties)
- In prefix for blocks: \p{InBasicLatin}, \p{InCyrillic}, etc.
- Keyword forms: \p{script=Latin}, \p{sc=Latin}, \p{block=BasicLatin},
  \p{blk=BasicLatin}, \p{general_category=Lu}, \p{gc=Lu}
- Case-insensitive script/block name resolution matching JDK behavior

New files:
- UnicodeProperties.java: binary property ranges (computed from JVM's
  Character class), Unicode block ranges, case-insensitive lookup index
- UnicodePropertySyntaxTest.java: 110 tests including cross-validation
  against JDK Pattern

Part of #112

commit.message:
Use LifetimeHolder for COM interop holders (#126522)

## Summary
This PR continues the CoreCLR holder cleanup by migrating remaining
`Wrapper<...>`-based RAII types to the newer holder abstractions.

- convert CLRConfig string buffers, `CRITSEC` allocations,
loader/resource helpers, GC/object handles, and COM interop temporaries
to `LifetimeHolder`, `ReleaseHolder`, or `SpecializedWrapper`
- replace bespoke holder classes and no-op helpers with traits-based
aliases, trimming boilerplate in `holder.h`, `wrappers.h`, and the
affected VM headers
- update transfer-of-ownership sites to use `Detach()` consistently and
remove a few additional unused methods/helpers surfaced by the
refactoring
- keep the refactoring behavior-preserving while tightening the debug
check for taking the address of a non-empty `LifetimeHolder`

commit.message:
fix: resolve compile error, CI warnings, and RwLock poisoning in Registration (#143)

* 🔒 [security fix] Fix unsafe pointer conversion in GlobalRules

Replace unsafe Arc-to-mut pointer casting in Registration with RwLock.
This eliminates undefined behavior when inserting rules during initialization
or concurrent access.

To ensure memory safety with RwLock, all variable collection methods
(defined_vars, defined_node_vars, get_local_util_vars) now return
RapidSet<String> instead of RapidSet<&str>. This prevents references from
dangling once the read lock is released.

🎯 What: Unsafe pointer conversion to mutable Map in GlobalRules.
⚠️ Risk: Undefined behavior and potential memory corruption during rule registration.
🛡️ Solution: Use RwLock for synchronized access and return owned Strings for variable sets.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🔒 [security fix] Address PR feedback and optimize RwLock usage

- Switched to an ArcSwap-like pattern (`Arc<RwLock<Arc<RapidMap>>>`) to reduce lock contention.
- Centralized lock access into `read()` and `update()` helper methods.
- Restored `.expect()` in `insert_rewriter` to ensure errors are surfaced.
- Maintained the memory-safe `RapidSet<String>` return types for variable collection.

🎯 What: Refined the security fix for unsafe pointer conversion.
⚠️ Risk: Reduced contention and improved error handling compared to the initial fix.
🛡️ Solution: Use an Arc-swapping pattern to release locks early while maintaining thread safety.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Adam Poulemanos <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Adam Poulemanos <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🔒 [security fix] Refine variable set types and resolve PR feedback

- Updated test helpers to use `RapidSet<String>` instead of `RapidSet<&str>` to match the new safe API.
- Fixed type mismatches in `Ctx` construction and `CheckHint::Rewriter` calls.
- Corrected `.contains()` usage in `check_var_in_fix`.

🎯 What: Finalized the security fix for unsafe pointer conversion.
🛡️ Solution: Consistent use of owned strings for variable tracking to ensure safety across RwLock access.

Co-authored-by: bashandbone <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🔒 [security fix] Address PR feedback and optimize API surface

- Finalized the `ArcSwap`-style model (`Arc<RwLock<Arc<RapidMap>>>`) to ensure low contention.
- Added `contains_key` to `Registration` and `has_global` to `RuleRegistration` to facilitate validation without full retrieval.
- Ensured all internal and public call sites are properly handled and type-consistent.
- Maintained memory safety by returning owned `RapidSet<String>` for variable tracking.

🎯 What: Security fix for unsafe pointer conversion with optimized concurrency.
🛡️ Solution: Low-contention safe access to shared rules with refined API helpers.

fix: agent forgets context on follow-up turns (history corruption)

Root cause: decomposePrompt() and dmTurnPrompt() wrappers (400+ token
instruction templates) were stored in history as "user" messages. On
turn 2, the model saw a massive system-like instruction in history instead
of the original human message — it couldn't connect the follow-up to the
prior conversation and reported "I don't have full context".

Example failure (from log):
  Turn 1 user history: "You are the Lead agent in a team channel.
    A message has arrived: --- <original task> --- Decide how to respond..."
  Turn 2 model: "I don't have context about what was discussed before"

Fixes:
1. RunningAgent.InjectHistoryEntry(raw): new method that pre-sets the
   content stored in history for the NEXT TurnStream call, decoupling
   what the model infers on (augmented) from what it remembers (raw).
   pendingHistoryEntry is consumed and cleared in TurnStream.

2. TurnStream: checks pendingHistoryEntry before appending to history;
   uses it when set, falls back to userContent otherwise.

3. HandleBossCommand: calls o.lead.InjectHistoryEntry(command) before
   every TurnStream so history contains only the raw user command —
   not the decomposePrompt wrapper. Also improved the follow-up prompt:
   removed confusing "proceed with task decomposition" and replaced with
   "proceed with the original request" which works with the now-clean
   history as context.

4. HandleDirectMessage: calls ra.InjectHistoryEntry(message) before
   TurnStream so dmTurnPrompt wrappers don't accumulate in history across
   the multi-turn DM conversation.

fix: isolate git test suite from user/system config to prevent hangs

The internal/git test suite timed out (600s) because git subprocesses
inherited the user's global git config - specifically LFS filter-process
(filter.lfs.required=true) and editor settings (core.editor=code --wait).
With many parallel tests spawning git processes, these caused indefinite
hangs.

Fix with two-layer isolation:
- TestMain sets GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM, GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL, and
  GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT at process level so production Client.run() calls
  are also isolated during tests
- testGitEnv() helper provides a clean environment for direct
  exec.Command calls in test helpers, with proper key deduplication
- initTestRepo configures commit.gpgsign=false and tag.gpgsign=false
  in local repo config as additional safety

commit.message:
Add EXP-1401: Clarke evaluation of PKGroupedEncoder on verification data

Evaluates the production champion (EXP-619 PKGroupedEncoder) on held-out
verification data with proper Clarke Error Grid analysis at all horizons.

Key design decisions:
- Uses verification split (not training) for honest evaluation
- Runs all 3 routed windows (w48, w96, w144) per patient
- 5-seed ensemble with ISF normalization (matching training)
- Applies routing table to aggregate best window per horizon
- Reports per-patient, per-horizon Clarke zones + MAE/RMSE/MARD

This replaces the EXP-929 Clarke evaluation which used Ridge regression
(MAE=27.3) with a direct evaluation of the production model (MAE~14).

Co-authored-by: QilongTang <3942418+QilongTang@users.noreply.github.com>

* Render bug fix

  Root cause: The branch replaced ReactDOM.render() (React 16 legacy, synchronous) with React 18's
  createRoot().render(), which is asynchronous. The LibraryContainer registers its handler callbacks (e.g.,
  refreshLibraryViewHandler) during its render. With the old synchronous API, the component mounted before
  setLoadedTypesJson() / refreshLibraryView() were called in createLibraryByElementId. With
  createRoot().render(), the render is scheduled but not yet executed — so all three handler calls are no-ops
  (handlers are still null). The component then mounted showing the empty placeholder with no way to recover.

  Fix: Wrap the initial root.render() call in flushSync() (src/entry-point.tsx), which forces synchronous
  rendering — exactly the same behavior as ReactDOM.render(). This ensures the handlers are registered before
  the data is passed.

* Bump npm package version from 1.0.9 to 1.1.0

fix: use cached masternode list in validate_pool_distribution

Replace list_all().await (async RwLock) with all_masternodes_cached()
(sync parking_lot cache) in validate_pool_distribution to prevent
tokio worker stall during fork resolution / reorg loops.

The async lock was contending with gossip and block production tasks
that hold the write lock, blocking the reorg tokio::spawn worker and
starving the runtime under load.

commit.message:
feat: autonomous issue implementation agent (#35)

## Summary

Adds the autonomous agent feature — the bot can pick up GitHub issues
labeled `agent`, implement them via Copilot CLI, create PRs, get
reviewed by the existing review bot, and iterate on feedback until
approved (max 3 iterations).

### Phase 2: Implementation feature
- **Implement skill** — `.github/skills/implement/SKILL.md` instructs
Copilot CLI to make changes without committing
- **`implement.py`** — orchestrator with `implement_issue()` +
`fix_pr()` (deep module, narrow interface)
- **Endpoints** — `POST /implement`, `POST /fix`, `GET
/implement/{issue_number}` with 202 background pattern
- **`git.py` expanded** — `create_branch_worktree()`,
`commit_and_push()`, `cleanup_branch_worktree()`
- **`github.py` expanded** — `get_issue()`, `get_pr()`,
`create_pull_request()`, `comment_on_issue()`

### Phase 3: Workflows + docs
- **`implement.yaml`** — triggers on `agent` label or `/implement`
comment
- **`implement-fix.yaml`** — auto-fixes on bot REQUEST_CHANGES (max 3
iterations)
- **`requirements.md`** — P9, P11, R21, R22, R26 → done; R24, R25 → in
progress

### Tests
- 32 tests passing (+9 new: implement orchestrator, fix orchestrator,
all endpoint tests)
- Lint, typecheck, format all green

### Pre-deploy requirement
GitHub App `homelab-review-bot` needs updated permissions:
- **Contents: write** (for `git push`)
- **Issues: read** (for fetching issue details)

### How it works
1. Label an issue `agent` → `implement.yaml` fires
2. Workflow calls `POST /implement` on the beelink agent
3. Agent: fetch issue → create `agent/issue-N` branch → Copilot CLI →
commit → create PR → comment `/review`
4. Review bot reviews the PR
5. If REQUEST_CHANGES → `implement-fix.yaml` fires → `POST /fix` →
iterate (max 3)
6. If APPROVE → ready for human merge

---------

commit.message:
Add \h, \H, \v, \V predefined character classes (#117)

Implement JDK-compatible horizontal and vertical whitespace character
classes:
- \h matches horizontal whitespace (tab, space, NBSP, Unicode spaces)
- \H matches non-horizontal whitespace (negation of \h)
- \v matches vertical whitespace (LF, VT, FF, CR, NEL, LS, PS)
- \V matches non-vertical whitespace (negation of \v)

Changes:
- Add HORIZ_SPACE and VERT_SPACE range tables to UnicodeTables
- Add \h and \v entries to PERL_GROUPS map
- Handle h/H/v/V cases in maybeParsePerlCCEscape()
- Remove \v literal escape from parseEscape() (now a char class)
- Add comprehensive tests with JDK cross-validation

The \v change fixes wrong semantics: previously \v was treated as
literal U+000B (vertical tab), but JDK treats it as a character class
matching [\n\x0B\f\r\x85\u2028\u2029].

Part of #112

commit.message:
Fix out_of_sync_recovery to iterate SCP slots directly

out_of_sync_recovery was using SlotQuorumTracker (capped at 12 slots)
to find v-blocking slots, but needs 100 decrements to trigger a purge.
Since 12 < 100, the purge condition could never be satisfied, making
the entire recovery path dead code.

Switch to iterating the SCP driver's unbounded slot map via
process_slots_descending_from + got_v_blocking, matching stellar-core's
outOfSyncRecovery which uses processSlotsDescendingFrom(UINT64_MAX).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(gateway): fix extension type identity mismatch in AssemblyLoadContext

Extension tools (exec, process) were not being discovered because
the custom AssemblyLoadContext loaded its own copy of shared
assemblies (BotNexus.AgentCore, Providers.Core, etc.), creating
a type identity mismatch — the extension's IAgentTool was a
different type than the host's.

Fix: return null from Load() for known host assemblies so they
resolve from the default context, preserving type identity for
interfaces like IAgentTool, IHookHandler, etc.
